questions.text: Under the Married Women's Property (MWP) Act, 1874, who can legally be the beneficiaries of a life insurance policy?
options: Only the parents of the husband
Wife alone, child/children alone, or wife and children jointly
The business partners of the husband
questions.explanation: Under Section 6 of the MWP Act, a married man can take a policy on his own life specifically for the benefit of his wife, his children, or both. No one else can be a beneficiary.

questions.text: What is the most distinguishing feature of a Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP) compared to traditional endowment policies?
options: Complete transparency and unbundling of various charges
It offers zero life risk cover
The premium is paid only after maturity
It guarantees a fixed bonus every year regardless of market performance
questions.explanation: ULIPs are fully transparent. The insurance company 'unbundles' the premium and clearly shows how much money is deducted for mortality, administration, and how much is actually invested in the funds.

questions.text: While life insurance protects a family against the financial risk of dying too early, an Annuity (Pension) protects a person against the risk of:
options: Living too long and running out of money
questions.explanation: The fundamental purpose of an annuity is to provide a guaranteed regular income until death, thereby eliminating the risk of a person outliving their retirement savings.

questions.text: Who is the proposer and premium payer in a Keyman Insurance policy?
questions.explanation: In Keyman Insurance, the employer (company) proposes the policy, pays the premiums, and is the sole beneficiary to protect the business from financial loss if their most valuable employee dies.

questions.text: Which specific section of the Married Women's Property Act, 1874 allows for the creation of an immediate trust for the family?
questions.explanation: Section 6 of the MWP Act allows a married man to buy a policy that legally becomes a trust for the benefit of his wife and children from the moment the policy is issued.

questions.text: In a Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP), who bears the investment risk of the market?
questions.explanation: Unlike traditional policies where the insurer bears the risk and guarantees returns, in a ULIP, the policyholder chooses the investment funds (equity/debt) and fully bears the market risk.

questions.text: An annuity where the regular pension payouts start immediately (usually the next month or year) after paying a single lump-sum premium is called:
questions.explanation: Immediate Annuities are designed for people who have just retired, have a lump sum of money, and want their pension payouts to begin instantly.

questions.text: As per IRDAI guidelines, what is the maximum Sum Assured generally allowed under a Micro-Insurance life policy?
questions.explanation: Micro-insurance is designed for economically vulnerable sections. To keep it accessible, regulations cap the maximum life cover (Sum Assured) at Rs. 2,00,000.

questions.text: How is the death claim amount received by a company under a Keyman Insurance policy treated for tax purposes?
options: It is completely tax-free for the company
It is transferred to the Keyman's family tax-free
It is fully taxable as business income
questions.explanation: Because the premiums paid by the company are claimed as a business expense to save tax during the policy term, any claim amount received upon the Keyman's death is treated as taxable business profit.

questions.text: The daily value of one unit in a ULIP fund, which indicates how the fund is performing, is determined by its:
questions.explanation: NAV (Net Asset Value) represents the current market price of one unit of the ULIP fund. Policyholders track the NAV to see if their investments are growing.

questions.text: Once a policy is endorsed under the MWP Act, the policyholder (husband) loses complete control over it because it legally becomes a:
questions.explanation: The moment an MWP policy is issued, a 'Trust' is created. The husband no longer owns the policy; the Trustees manage it exclusively for the benefit of the wife/children.

questions.text: In a Deferred Annuity, the early period during which the policyholder pays regular premiums to build their retirement corpus is called the:
questions.explanation: A Deferred Annuity has two phases: The Accumulation Phase (where you save and build the fund) and the Vesting/Payout Phase (where the company starts paying you the pension).

questions.text: What is the biggest legal advantage of buying a life insurance policy under the MWP Act?
options: The policy matures in just 5 years
The wife can sell the policy to a bank
The policy money cannot be attached by the husband's creditors or court orders
questions.explanation: Because the policy is a Trust for the family, it is immune from the husband's debts. Even if the husband goes bankrupt, creditors and courts cannot touch the MWP policy funds.

questions.text: The specific age at which the policyholder's deferment period ends and they start receiving regular pension payouts is known as the:
questions.explanation: In pension/annuity plans, the term 'Maturity' is replaced by 'Vesting'. The Vesting Age is the exact age when the pension payments begin.

questions.text: The facility to withdraw a certain percentage (e.g., up to 60%) of the accumulated pension corpus as a lump sum at the time of retirement is called:
questions.explanation: Commutation allows a retiring person to take a chunk of their pension fund as a tax-free lump sum to meet immediate needs, while the remaining amount is used to buy a regular monthly annuity.

questions.text: The facility in a ULIP that allows the policyholder to shift their money from an aggressive equity fund to a safe debt fund is known as:
questions.explanation: Fund switching is a highly flexible ULIP feature. It allows investors to move their accumulated money between different funds (equity, debt, balanced) based on market conditions.

questions.text: Who among the following is permitted to act as a Micro-Insurance Agent under IRDAI regulations?
options: Only individual brokers from metro cities
questions.explanation: To penetrate rural and low-income areas, IRDAI allows NGOs, SHGs, and Micro-Finance Institutions (MFIs) to act as agents and distribute micro-insurance policies.

questions.text: Can a policyholder take a loan against a life insurance policy issued under the MWP Act?
options: Yes, easily up to 90% of surrender value
Yes, but only to expand his business
No, neither a loan can be taken nor can the policy be surrendered for the husband's own use
Yes, if the insurance agent permits it
questions.explanation: Since the husband does not own the policy (it belongs to the trust), he cannot pledge it for a personal or business loan, nor can he surrender it to get cash for himself.

questions.text: What happens if a 'Keyman' resigns from the company before the Keyman Insurance policy matures?
options: The policy is automatically transferred to the Keyman's wife
The company can choose to surrender the policy or assign it to the departing Keyman
The policy automatically becomes a ULIP
questions.explanation: If the employee leaves, the company has two main choices: surrender the policy and take the cash value, or legally assign the policy to the resigning employee (who then pays future premiums).

questions.text: Which specific charge is deducted from a ULIP fund strictly to cover the cost of the life insurance protection (death risk)?
questions.explanation: The Mortality Charge is the actual cost of providing the life cover. It is deducted from the fund value every month based on the policyholder's age and the amount at risk.

questions.text: To buy a life insurance policy under the Married Women's Property (MWP) Act, when must the proposer fill out the MWP addendum form?
options: Just before the claim is filed
Any time before the policy matures
At the time of taking a loan on the policy
Only at the time of making the initial proposal
questions.explanation: An MWP trust must be created at the very inception of the policy. The proposer must submit the MWP addendum form along with the main proposal form. An existing normal policy cannot be converted into an MWP policy later.

questions.text: In a ULIP, if the policyholder decides to completely surrender the policy within the mandatory 5-year lock-in period, what happens to their money?
options: It is refunded immediately to their bank account
It is permanently seized by the insurance company
It is moved to a 'Discontinued Policy Fund' and paid only after the 5-year lock-in ends
It is converted into a traditional endowment plan
questions.explanation: ULIPs have a strict 5-year lock-in. If surrendered early, the risk cover stops, and the fund value is shifted to a Discontinued Fund. The money is paid out only after the 5th policy anniversary.

questions.text: Which of the following conditions is absolutely necessary for a company to buy a Keyman Insurance policy on an employee?
options: The employee must be related to the owner
The employer must demonstrate a clear financial loss to the business if the Keyman dies
The employee must have a minimum salary of 1 Crore
The company must be a government entity
questions.explanation: To establish Insurable Interest, the employer must prove to the underwriter that the death of this specific employee would cause a severe loss of profit or business disruption.

questions.text: The main objective of introducing Micro-Insurance regulations in India was to:
options: Help rich investors save more tax
Provide a platform for stock market trading
Offer high-value life insurance to NRIs
Provide affordable insurance protection to economically vulnerable sections and the rural poor
questions.explanation: Micro-insurance is specifically regulated to promote financial inclusion by offering low-cost, small-ticket insurance policies to people below the poverty line and in rural sectors.

questions.text: If a husband takes a policy under the MWP Act but forgets to name specific 'Trustees' in the form, who automatically acts as the trustee by default law?
options: The Official Trustee of the State
questions.explanation: By law, if no specific trustees are appointed by the policyholder in the MWP form, the Official Trustee of the respective State automatically becomes the trustee to safeguard the family's interests.
